content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
The Honorable Art Torres 
state Senator 
Room 2080, State Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Senator Torres: 
May 10, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-l76 
You have requested advice regarding the conflict of interest 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.ll This letter 
will confirm the advice provided by telephone to Mr. Bob Morales 
of your office. 
FACTS 
As chairman of the Joint Committee on Refugee Resettlement, 
International Migration and Cooperative Development, you are 
planning to lead a delegation to vietnam. 
Because of limited 
government funding, the trip will be paid for by donations from 
the public. 
You have set up a separate bank account to receive 
the donations and to pay the expenses of the trip. 
The costs of 
the trip will be more than $50. 
However, most donations will be 
in relatively small amounts, some as low as $10. 
In a telephone 
conversation, Mr. Morales of your office informed me that any 
money left over after the trip expenses are paid would not be used 
for "political purposes." 
Such money probably would be returned 
to the donors or donated to a charitable organization. 
II Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


The Honorable Art Torres 
May 10, 1989 
Page Two 
QUESTION 
What are the reporting requirements in connection with the 
donations you receive to pay for the trip? 
CONCLUSION 
You must disclose on your statement of economic interests 
(Form 721) specified information concerning any person who donates 
$50 or more for the trip. 
In addition, even if no single source 
donates $50 or more, because the total value of the trip will be 
more than $50 you must disclose specified information about the 
trip on your statement of economic interests. 
ANALYSIS 
The donations received to pay for the trip are "gifts" 
pursuant to Section 82028. 
(See also Regulation 18228, copy 
enclosed.) 
Elected officers are required to dislcose any gift(s) 
received from a single source, if the aggregate value of gift(s) 
received during a calendar year is $50 or more. 
In addition, Regulation 18726.6 provides: 
A gift which is received from multiple donors must 
be disclosed if the gift's value equals or exceeds 
$50. 
The names of donors who individually have given 
less than $50 need not be separately disclosed; it is 
sufficient to describe in general terms those who gave 
the gift. 
If, however, any donor or his or her agent 
or intermediary gave $50 or more, his or her name must 
be disclosed. 
Therefore, even if you do not receive $50 or more from a 
single source, certain information about the trip must be 
disclosed on Schedule G of your annual statement of economic 
interests (Form 721). 
You should note that the funds donated may be considered 
contributions to you if used for political purposes. 
(Section 


The Honorable Art Torres 
May 10, 1989 
Page Three 
82015; Regulation 18215, copy enclosed.) 
Please refer to 
Regulation 18228 for guidance regarding the distinction between 
"gifts" for travel and "contributions" for political travel. 
Please call me at 322-5662 if you have any questions about 
this letter. 
, 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
l~e~/1t 
By: 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division

March 20, 1989 
Ms. Jeanne Pritchard, Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
"JfiGHBOF~H(X)O V10LLNCf 
>t,-l::cTC(~MMfnLl (';"'4 
TOURISM AND AV1ATiC)~ 
CHA1I=?MAN, 
SUBCOM.....,lTICE.3 HE"AL TH 
HUMAN SERVICES 8:: ALRB 
SUECOMMITTEE ON 
V!C~lMS RiGHTS (CHAiRMAN f 
Re: 
REQUEST FOR CONFIRMING TELEPHONE ADVICE; RESPONSE NEEDED 
BEFORE MARCH 24, 1989. 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
I am writing to confirm the appropriate application of 
Regulation 18726 et seq with respect to the following 
circumstances. 
I am Chairman of the Joint Committee on Refugee Resettlement, 
International Migration and Cooperative Development. 
In that 
capacity, I plan to lead a delegation to Vietnam. 
The objectives 
of the trip are to advocate for refugee emigration from Vietnam 
through the Orderly Departure Program. 
Specifically, the 
delegation will focus on humanitarian issues of priority for both 
the Vietnamese-Americans in California and for the United States 
government. 
Because of limited government funding, the costs for my trip 
will be provided by donations received from numerous members of 
the Vietnamese community from throughout the state. 
In order to 
facilitate their donations, I have established an independent 
bank account, administered by a C.P.A., to which the donations 
can be made. 
While the trip will cost me several thousand 
dollars, individual donations will be generally much less than 
$1000, with no donation in excess of that amount. 
In fact, most 
donations will be in relatively small amounts, some as low as 
$10. 


-
2 
Gifts to support my trip will be identified by the donor as 
being for the Family Reunification Project. 
Records will be 
maintained for purposes of disclosure on my annual Statement of 
Economic Interests. 
Records of disbursements will also be 
maintained and will identify the nature of the expense and the 
payee. 
A legislative staff person will accompany me on the trip. 
The costs for the staff person's travel will be handled 
separately. 
Am I correct in my understanding that pursuant to Regulation 
18726 et seq I must disclose those donors whose contribution 
equals or exceeds $50 (when combined with any other gift received' 
from the same donor during the calendar year -- which is highly 
improbable with respect to these donors). 
As for other donors, I 
need not disclose them by name, but may instead indicate that 
numerous members of the Vietnamese community contributed to the 
cost of the trip. 
Please confirm that the proposed management and reporting of 
these gifts will comply with the requirements of the Political 
Reform Act as interpreted by regulation and opinion. 
And if 
there is a question of compliance, please advise what steps 
should be taken with respect to collecting donations for this 
upcoming trip. 
Because the trip will begin on March 29, 1989, I request that 
you provide me with a prompt telephone confirmation, to be 
followed up by letter in the normal course of your work. 
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact 
Ms. Beverly Hunter-Curtis of my s 
ff a 
323-8641. 
AT:hbhc 
ART TORRES 
Senator
